content.text: Email Integrations

Email integrations connect an agent to an inbox so it can triage incoming mail, draft replies, extract structured data from messages, and take downstream actions (create tickets, update CRM records, forward to the right team). The core challenge is email parsing — messages arrive as HTML or plain-text with quoted history, attachments, and inconsistent formatting — so a preprocessing step cleans the input before the LLM sees it.

attrs.alt: Email agent integration: inbound email → preprocessor → agent (classify, draft, tool calls) → actions → human review gate; thread store

content.text: Receiving Email: IMAP Polling

The simplest way to receive email in Python is IMAP polling: connect to the inbox periodically, fetch unseen messages, process each one, and mark it as seen (or move it to a processed label). This approach works with any email provider that supports IMAP — Gmail, Outlook, custom mail servers.
Receiving Email via Webhook (Mailgun)

Polling works for low-volume inboxes but introduces latency. Mailgun's Inbound Routes and SendGrid's Inbound Parse deliver emails as HTTP POST webhooks within seconds of arrival — no polling required.

# pip install fastapi uvicorn anthropic
from fastapi import FastAPI, Form, Request
import json
from anthropic import Anthropic

app = FastAPI()
client = Anthropic()

@app.post("/webhooks/mailgun/inbound")
async def mailgun_inbound(
    request: Request,
    sender: str = Form(...),
    recipient: str = Form(...),
    subject: str = Form(default=""),
    body_plain: str = Form(default="", alias="body-plain"),
    stripped_text: str = Form(default="", alias="stripped-text"),
) -> dict:
    # stripped_text is the body without quoted history — prefer it
    content = stripped_text or body_plain

    # Trim to 2000 chars to keep the prompt manageable
    trimmed = content[:2000]

    resp = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=300,
        messages=[{
            "role": "user",
            "content": (
                f"Email from: {sender}\nSubject: {subject}\n\n{trimmed}\n\n"
                "Draft a polite, professional reply in 3-4 sentences."
            )
        }]
    )
    draft_reply = resp.content[0].text
    print(f"Draft for {sender}:\n{draft_reply}")
    # In production: store the draft and notify a human to review and send
    return {"status": "draft_created", "draft": draft_reply}
Stripping HTML and Quoted History

# pip install beautifulsoup4 html2text
import re
import html2text
from bs4 import BeautifulSoup

def clean_email_body(raw: str, is_html: bool = False) -> str:
    """Strip HTML, CSS, and quoted reply history from an email body.""""
    if is_html:
        # Convert HTML to clean plain text
        converter = html2text.HTML2Text()
        converter.ignore_links = True
        converter.ignore_images = True
        raw = converter.handle(raw)

    lines = raw.splitlines()
    cleaned = []
    for line in lines:
        stripped = line.strip()
        # Remove quoted lines (">", "On Mon, ... wrote:", "-----Original Message-----")
        if stripped.startswith(">"):
            continue
        if re.match(r"^On .+ wrote:$", stripped):
            break  # Stop at the quoted reply separator
        if "-----Original Message-----" in stripped:
            break
        cleaned.append(line)

    # Collapse multiple blank lines into one
    text = "\n".join(cleaned)
    text = re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", text)
    return text.strip()

# Example
raw = """
Hi Alice,

Thanks for reaching out. I'll look into that invoice issue.

On Mon, 1 Jul 2025, Alice wrote:
> Hi Bob,
> Could you check invoice #1042?
"""
print(clean_email_body(raw))
# Output:
# Hi Alice,
# Thanks for reaching out. I'll look into that invoice issue.
Drafting and Sending Replies

After the agent drafts a reply, it should go through a human review gate before sending — especially for customer-facing email. The standard pattern is to save the draft to a database and notify a human to approve it. On approval, use SMTP or a transactional email API to send.

# pip install sendgrid anthropic
import os
from sendgrid import SendGridAPIClient
from sendgrid.helpers.mail import Mail
from anthropic import Anthropic

client = Anthropic()
sg = SendGridAPIClient(os.environ["SENDGRID_API_KEY"])

def draft_reply(original: dict) -> str:
    resp = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=400,
        system=(
            "You are a helpful customer support agent for Acme Corp. "
            "Write professional, concise replies. Sign off as 'Acme Support Team'."
        ),
        messages=[{
            "role": "user",
            "content": (
                f"Customer email:\nFrom: {original['from']}\n"
                f"Subject: {original['subject']}\n"
                f"Body:\n{original['body']}\n\n"
                "Write a reply."
            )
        }]
    )
    return resp.content[0].text

def send_reply(to_email: str, subject: str, body: str) -> bool:
    msg = Mail(
        from_email="support@acme-corp.com",
        to_emails=to_email,
        subject=f"Re: {subject}",
        plain_text_content=body,
    )
    response = sg.send(msg)
    return response.status_code == 202

# Example flow:
# original = {"from": "alice@example.com", "subject": "Invoice #1042", "body": "..."}
# draft = draft_reply(original)
# print(f"DRAFT:\n{draft}")
# # Human reviews; on approval:
# sent = send_reply(original["from"], original["subject"], draft)
Email Triage with Routing

For high-volume inboxes, the agent classifies each message and routes it to the right team queue rather than attempting to reply immediately. The classification drives the action — low-confidence results get flagged for human review.
Thread Continuity
Email conversations span multiple messages. The agent needs the full thread context to give coherent replies. Use the email's
In-Reply-To
or
Message-ID
headers to link replies to their thread, and store the thread history in Redis or a database.

import redis, json, email as email_lib
from anthropic import Anthropic

r = redis.Redis(host="localhost", port=6379, decode_responses=True)
client = Anthropic()

def get_thread_key(message_id: str) -> str:
    return f"email_thread:{message_id}"

def load_thread(thread_id: str) -> list[dict]:
    raw = r.get(get_thread_key(thread_id))
    return json.loads(raw) if raw else []

def save_thread(thread_id: str, messages: list[dict]) -> None:
    r.set(get_thread_key(thread_id), json.dumps(messages), ex=86400 * 7)  # 7-day TTL

def reply_in_thread(parsed_email: dict) -> str:
    # Resolve which thread this belongs to
    in_reply_to = parsed_email.get("in_reply_to") or parsed_email.get("message_id")
    thread = load_thread(in_reply_to)
    thread.append({"role": "user", "content": parsed_email["body"]})

    resp = client.messages.create(
        model="claude-haiku-4-5-20251001",
        max_tokens=400,
        system="You are a support agent. Reply to the latest message, keeping the full thread in mind.",
        messages=thread,
    )
    reply = resp.content[0].text
    thread.append({"role": "assistant", "content": reply})
    save_thread(in_reply_to, thread)
    return reply
Anti-Spam and Safety Guards
Verify sender domain
— do not auto-reply to messages from disposable email domains. Check the domain against a blocklist before running the agent.
Limit auto-send to known senders
— automatically send replies only to contacts already in your CRM; flag unknown senders for human review.
Rate-limit per sender
— a sender who submits 100 emails in 5 minutes is likely testing or attacking. Apply a per-sender rate limit and reject excess.
Never echo sensitive data
— the agent's reply might accidentally include data from other customers if the prompt is poorly constructed. Use system prompt isolation and test with adversarial inputs.
Archive all agent actions
— store every draft, approval decision, and sent email with timestamps for compliance and debugging.
Summary

Email integrations feed messages into an agent via IMAP polling or webhook delivery (Mailgun, SendGrid). A preprocessing step strips HTML, CSS, and quoted history before the body reaches the LLM. The agent classifies intent, routes messages to the right team, or drafts a reply — but should send auto-replies only after a human review gate for customer-facing email. Thread continuity is maintained via the
In-Reply-To
header and a Redis-backed thread store. Apply per-sender rate limits, domain blocklists, and action audit logs to keep the integration safe and compliant.
